commit.message:
fix: robust ACP executable resolution with multi-strategy fallback

Replace fragile where/which-only validation in AcpAdapter with
multi-strategy path resolution via acpPathResolver.ts:

1. Check absolute path existence (with Windows .cmd/.exe extension)
2. Try system PATH via where/which
3. Scan common npm/node install locations per platform:
   - Windows: C:\.tools\.npm-global, %APPDATA%\npm, etc.
   - macOS: /usr/local/bin, /opt/homebrew/bin, etc.
   - Linux: ~/.npm-global/bin, /usr/local/bin, etc.

When found via fallback scan, auto-updates adapter executable to
resolved absolute path. Session-level cache avoids repeated lookups.
Failure includes full diagnostic report (checked paths, existence).

Also solidified troubleshooting knowledge into:
- system-instructions.md: ACP Engine Troubleshooting section
- mcp-servers.json: PATH inheritance documentation
- Project memory: architecture-decision entry

9 new tests, all passing.

Fixes #555

commit.message:
security: harden checkout steps and fix module verification

- Add persist-credentials: false to all three checkout steps
  (govulncheck, gosec, trivy) to prevent the GITHUB_TOKEN from
  persisting in the git config, reducing credential exposure.

- Replace misleading 'Verify go.sum' step (which silently succeeds on
  an empty module cache) with 'Download and verify modules' that runs
  go mod download && go mod verify, ensuring checksums are actually
  validated against go.sum.

- Add the same download-and-verify step to the gosec job since CI jobs
  run on isolated runners with independent caches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: prompt cache optimization for parallel worker spawns

Split monolithic prompts into shared prefix + unique suffix to leverage
LLM API prompt caching. When multiple workers are spawned in parallel
(e.g. council dispatch), the shared prefix (system instructions, memory,
project context) is cached once and reused across all workers.

Implementation:
- PromptCacheManager: splits at 'Task Description:' boundary, SHA256
  cache key, TTL eviction (30min), hit tracking with stats
- AcpAdapter: sends multi-block content with cache_control ephemeral
  on shared prefix, with triple fallback chain (multi-block → single
  block → text string) for backward compatibility
- genericExecutor: passes promptParts through ExecuteOptions
- worker-spawner: splits basePrompt and passes promptParts to executor

Typical savings: 10-15KB shared prefix cached across 6 council members,
only ~0.5-1KB unique suffix processed per additional worker.

Refs #555

feat: lifecycle hooks system for task execution pipeline

Add a lifecycle hook registry inspired by Claude Code's hook architecture.
Hooks fire at 16 strategic points across the execution pipeline:

Task Lifecycle (council-runner.ts):
- PreTaskSpawn, TaskStarted, PreDelegation, PostDelegation
- PreSynthesis, TaskCompleted

Execution Phases (worker-spawner.ts):
- PreRoleResolution, PreSkillLoad, PrePromptConstruct
- PreExecute, PostExecute, ValidationGate, LoopDetection

MCP Dispatch (mcp-server.ts):
- PreToolDispatch, PostToolDispatch

Key design decisions:
- Best-effort: hook errors are caught and logged, never block execution
- Fire-and-forget: hooks use .catch(() => {}) pattern
- Veto support: Pre* hooks can return { veto: true } to block operations
- Singleton registry via getGlobalHookRegistry()
- Built-in hooks: taskMetricsHook, slowExecutionHook, loggingHook

Refs #555

refactor: adopt Microsoft.Extensions.AI agent thread pattern for per-user conversations

Replace the manual ConversationTurn round-trip with a proper agent thread:

- GunnarBotPrompts.cs: extract system prompt to shared static class (breaks
  circular dependency, single source of truth for all prompt strings)

- ConversationCache: now stores List<ChatMessage> per user (the agent thread)
  - GetOrCreateThread(userId)  seeds fresh thread with system prompt
  - TouchThread(userId, thread)  slides cache expiry, enforces max history trim
  - ClearThread(userId)  resets to fresh thread on next access
  - Cache key changed to 'agent-thread:{userId}'

- QueryService: refactored HandleQueryAsync to accept the live thread
  - Builds an ephemeral call-time snapshot: [persistent thread] +
    [product context system message] + [user message]
  - Product context is NOT stored in the thread (keeps history clean)
  - Appends user message + assistant response via thread.AddMessages(response)
  - ConversationTurn record removed  no longer needed

- MessageHandlerService / SlashCommandService: updated to use
  GetOrCreateThread / TouchThread / ClearThread API
